title: Exploring Networking
description: Practical networking for SREs and platform engineers — DNS, load balancers, TLS, VPCs, and Kubernetes networking taught through real production incidents.

items.title: Home
items.content_html: Practical networking for SREs and platform engineers — how APIs get exposed and secured on the wire, plus DNS, load balancers, TLS, and Kubernetes networking.

items.title: Reverse Proxies and API Gateways, Demystified
items.content_html: Production APIs sit behind a front door. Learn how reverse proxies and API gateways handle TLS, routing, auth, and rate limiting before traffic hits you.

items.title: CORS Explained: The Front-End/Back-End Border
items.content_html: CORS is the browser rule that confuses every back-end dev. Learn why the browser blocks cross-origin calls and how the server actually grants permission.

items.title: From URL to Endpoint: How an API Gets Exposed
items.content_html: What does 'exposing an endpoint' actually mean? Follow a URL through DNS, ports, and the listening server to see where your API really lives.

items.title: HTTPS for APIs: Where the Connection Gets Secured
items.content_html: Where does HTTPS encryption actually stop, and what guards the hops after it? Learn TLS termination, how mTLS works, and why it's the 2026 standard.
